agent_notes: Brent has no PAINTS-approved Creative Walk route or park from the v6 safety list inside the borough; Creative Walks omitted rather than routed through unapproved parks.
The strongest PAINTS cluster is Queen's Park/Kensal Rise/Kensal Green; Wembley provides markets and public art but fewer independent, walk-in-friendly venues.
Several operational details, especially exact group walk-in tolerance and holiday opening, need host confirmation before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Wednesday 5pm-10pm, Thursday-Friday 4pm-11pm, Saturday 3pm-11pm; closed Monday, Tuesday and Sunday. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12; venue presents as neighbourhood wine bar/shop with reservations/contact available.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable off-peak with advance host call; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Small wine bar/shop; avoid peak Friday/Saturday without calling.
venues.why_fits_paints: A compact neighbourhood wine bar/shop with enough specificity for a show-and-tell bottle theme. College Road gives it a tucked-away local texture rather than destination-bar obviousness. It is best treated as an intimate PAINTS pick, not a large social table.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded/run by Tiane and Abbi per social presence; the address is part of the small Kensal Green/Kensal Rise independent strip rather than a central wine-bar circuit.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine by the glass/bottle and shop bottles; menu specifics vary.
venues.safety_notes: Residential high street close to Kensal Green and Kensal Rise transport; evening departure route should stay on College Road/Chamberlayne Road.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8 min walk via Chamberlayne Road / Station Terrace.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small footprint and limited opening days are the main operational risks.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists standard opening from 1pm Monday-Thursday, noon Friday, 11am Saturday-Sunday with evening closes varying by day; contact page lists shorter shop times, so exact bar hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Drop-in retail/wine bar model; group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible if using tasting/shop format; seated group capacity UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Better for a hosted tasting-style meet than a guaranteed single table.
venues.why_fits_paints: Independent bottle-shop energy, close to Kensal Rise station, and useful for people bringing one bottle or label story into the conversation. It has stronger local texture than polish. The opening-hours discrepancy needs a host check but the venue itself is PAINTS-coded.
venues.anecdote_story: It presents itself as 'the wine, beer and spirit of independence', a nice hook for a matched group built around personal taste and small producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, beer and spirits, with occasional drop-in tasting signalled on Instagram.
venues.safety_notes: Station Terrace is immediately by Kensal Rise Overground; straightforward exit route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Conflicting official opening-hour snippets; host must confirm the live schedule.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Monday-Saturday midday-11pm, Sunday midday-10:30pm; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format implies walk-ins; 8-12 group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable outside peak meal periods.
venues.capacity_notes: Larger pub footprint than nearby wine bars; still call before a PAINTS group.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper Kensal Rise pub with enough local identity to support a relaxed Show & Tell Drinks format. It is less rare than 116 or Rise & Vine, but operationally steadier. Good as a reliable anchor when smaller wine rooms are too fragile.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub sits on Chamberlayne Road, the old spine of Kensal Rise's independent food-and-drink strip.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, wine and food menu.
venues.safety_notes: High-street setting near Kensal Rise Overground; use lit Chamberlayne Road routes after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: More conventional pub atmosphere; avoid loud sports/peak dinner periods.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Monday-Thursday noon-11pm, Friday-Saturday noon-midnight, Sunday noon-11pm; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub/restaurant with booking options; group walk-in for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable, especially with the garden/private-hire spaces, but host should call.
venues.capacity_notes: Heated all-weather garden and pub dining areas give more operational slack than small bars.
venues.why_fits_paints: The venue is independent-local rather than chain-templated, with a quirky Kensal Rise texture and enough space for strangers to settle. It is a strong backup-to-primary pub for wine-led venues. The garden gives useful seasonal flexibility.
venues.anecdote_story: Local listings note details like a typewriter menu holder and board games, which gives the host something tactile to point to.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, wine, pub food and Sunday roast.
venues.safety_notes: Chamberlayne Road is active and close to Kensal Rise Overground; avoid late dispersal through quieter side streets.
venues.risks_watchouts: Urban Pubs & Bars group ownership reduces the independent-owner story slightly.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Monday-Saturday midday-11pm, Sunday midday-10:30pm; kitchen hours vary. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format implies walk-ins; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable off-peak; better as a roomy fallback to 116 WineBar & Shop.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional pub footprint, useful for a single table if contacted.
venues.why_fits_paints: A practical local pub on College Road, strong as an operationally robust Show & Tell Drinks host. It is not as distinctive as the smaller wine rooms, but it gives Brent a non-central, local-feeling anchor. Best used when conversation reliability matters more than novelty.
venues.anecdote_story: College Road and the Kensal Green rail-side setting give it neighbourhood specificity; no deeper owner story found.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and seasonal food menu.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Kensal Green; keep routes to the station on main roads after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: More conventional; PAINTS host should bring the theme/story.

venues.opening_hours: Official site states brunch daily and restaurant/bar service; exact daily close and bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Restaurant/bar with booking culture; walk-in group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm, therefore Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible with booking/heads-up, but less PAINTS-walk-in-pure.
venues.capacity_notes: Restaurant layout supports groups, but this is more dining-led than drinks-led.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 5pm-7pm for drinks before dinner peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Jesse Dunford Wood's eccentric British cooking gives it real story and character. For PAINTS, it works best as drinks-plus-snacks rather than a pure bar. It is capped at Tier B because group walk-ins are not clearly confirmed.
venues.anecdote_story: Established in 2012 by chef Jesse Dunford Wood; the self-description leans into eccentric, nostalgic Britishness.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cocktails, wine, brunch and British comfort food.
venues.safety_notes: Near Kensal Green station; use main-road route to/from the station after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: More restaurant than wine bar; could become meal-focused and pricier than PAINTS default.

venues.opening_hours: Official about page lists Monday-Tuesday 10am-8pm, Wednesday-Thursday 10am-9pm, Friday 10am-10pm, Saturday 10am-9pm, Sunday 10am-6pm; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Shop/bar model and Instagram says pop in; group seating for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible as standing/tasting format; seated group capacity uncertain.
venues.capacity_notes: Best for a tasting led by bottle selection; not a guaranteed long seated session.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of Brent's cleanest category fits: independent wine merchant, artisan food, and a clear local community role. The shop/bar hybrid gives the host easy prompts around regions, labels and producers. It is distinctive without being fussy.
venues.anecdote_story: Local guides describe the Winestore as part of the Salusbury Road food-and-drink cluster, focused on wine and run by local food and beverage experts.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, beer, spirits, cheeses, charcuterie and antipasti.
venues.safety_notes: Queen's Park high-street setting with tube/rail nearby; good for individual arrival and departure.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small venue; call to confirm format for a 2-hour group.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Monday 4pm-11pm, Tuesday-Wednesday noon-11pm, Thursday-Saturday noon-midnight, Sunday noon-5:30pm; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram says walk-ins welcome; 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable in bar area or off-peak dining room.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub/dining room with more space than the adjacent Winestore.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 6pm-8pm or Saturday late afternoon before dinner peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Queen's Park local with enough polish for strangers and enough pub soul to avoid a stiff dinner setup. It is a practical PAINTS anchor beside the stronger wine-store pick. The walk-in signal is better than most reservation-led restaurants in the area.
venues.anecdote_story: Reopened as a reimagined Queen's Park local by local residents according to listings; good host hook around neighbourhood continuity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, dining-room food and Sunday roast.
venues.safety_notes: Strong transit adjacency and active high-street frontage.
venues.risks_watchouts: Recent relaunch means host should confirm current operating rhythm and group policy.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says open from midday weekdays and 10am Saturday-Sunday; SquareMeal lists Tuesday-Friday noon-11pm, Saturday 10am-11pm, Sunday 10am-10pm. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official reservations page says space is kept for walk-ins during the week; groups of 8+ use set menu, therefore Tier B for PAINTS walk-in model.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only with planning or set-menu acceptance.
venues.capacity_notes: Lonsdale Road restaurant setting; likely busy at dinner.
venues.why_fits_paints: Beautiful room, strong Eastern Mediterranean food and wine, and a lively but warm Lonsdale Road setting. It bends the category because it is restaurant-led, not a wine bar. Keep as a Tier B social-drinks fallback, not a pure PAINTS flagship.
venues.anecdote_story: A Queen's Park offshoot from the Berber & Q family, with recent Reddit/food discussion noting the room and menu as memorable.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Eastern Mediterranean flatbreads, mezze, cocktails and wine.
venues.safety_notes: Active Queen's Park restaurant street near tube/rail.
venues.risks_watchouts: Group set menu and dinner demand make it operationally less flexible.

venues.opening_hours: London Farmers' Markets lists weekly rain-or-shine Sunday market; multiple listings cite Sunday 10am-2pm. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no individual booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a drifting small group; meet outside school entrance then split/reconvene.
venues.capacity_notes: School-yard market; busy but manageable for 8-12 if the host sets a clear meeting point.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Brent's best Market Days pick: producer-led, local, weekly, and proven. It has enough stalls for personal discovery without the shopping-centre feel. The Sunday rhythm gives the group a natural pace and conversation prompts.
venues.anecdote_story: London Farmers' Markets says it has run for 20 years and was voted best in the UK; useful host opener about producer stamina and neighbourhood loyalty.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Organic produce, meat, eggs, juice, cheese, bread, fish, pasta and seasonal food stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime school-yard/high-street environment; strong transit access.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying market backup within 15 min walk/transit; closest qualifying Brent alternative is Wembley Park Summer Market, around 25-35 min by transit. Primary kept Tier A because the market itself is operationally strong, but backup constraint is a caveat.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and stall mix vary; host should confirm weekly trader lineup.

venues.opening_hours: Official Wembley Park page lists 2026 summer market from 7 June to 27 September 2026 with dated market pages such as 21 June 2026 11am-7pm; exact July dates must be checked.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a daytime group, especially when paired with the public-art trail.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public square; crowds spike around stadium/arena events.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday late morning when no major stadium crowd is arriving.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer/artisan mix, street food and Wembley Park's visual public realm make this more interesting than a generic food court. It gives Brent geographic range beyond Queen's Park. Use it on non-event days to preserve the social pace.
venues.anecdote_story: Wembley Park positions the market as part of the district's shift from event-day destination to all-week neighbourhood.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm-reared and organic produce, vegan treats, Mediterranean goods, plants, organic wines, Italian deli goods, pies and artisan/lifestyle stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Large managed public realm, but avoid major stadium ingress/egress times for comfort.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying market backup within 15 min walk/transit; Queen's Park Farmers' Market is the closest qualifying Brent alternative, around 25-35 min by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Seasonal schedule and event-day crowding; host must check the exact date.

venues.opening_hours: Brent Council plan-your-visit page confirms free museum/exhibition space at the Library at Willesden Green; exact July 2026 opening hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum/library setting; timed-entry not indicated. Group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for quiet small group if host manages pacing.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum/gallery inside public library; avoid children's-event times if conversation matters.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning or Saturday early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: The borough museum gives Brent-specific story depth: Wembley Stadium, domestic life, migration and local archives. It is not a blockbuster exhibition venue, so it sits just under Tier A. Strong for a group that wants place rather than spectacle.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum collection holds more than 10,000 artefacts and reflects working and domestic life in Brent from about 1850 to the present.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local history objects, archives, oral histories and temporary exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Public civic building on Willesden High Road; daylight/early evening preferred.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition press within last three months is weak; host must verify live exhibition before use.

venues.opening_hours: Brent Council says the gallery is located within the Library at Willesden Green; exact exhibition opening hours and holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public gallery/library setting; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if exhibition is open and not under install/deinstall.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery in civic library; check installation dates.
venues.why_fits_paints: A true Brent visual-art space for emerging and established artists, which is rare in the borough. It needs a current exhibition hook to become more than a room visit. Kept Tier B because the live exhibition calendar was not robustly verifiable from web snippets.
venues.anecdote_story: Brent Council describes it as a premier North West London place for eclectic, high-quality art from established and emerging artists.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating art exhibitions; exact July 2026 show UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Civic library setting on a main road; suitable daytime/early evening.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition dates and install closures are the binding constraint.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists 1 Aug 2024-31 Dec 2028, free open-air art gallery; individual lighting/digital works may vary after dark.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access public art trail; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted visual prompt route, with clear meeting/reconvening points.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor public realm; crowd levels depend heavily on stadium/arena event calendar.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday late morning or weekday early evening on non-event days.
venues.why_fits_paints: A free open-air gallery with 26 works gives Brent a credible visual-art experience outside the Willesden civic hub. It is more urban public-art day than conventional museum day, but the works are concrete enough for discussion. Strongest when paired with a cafe meet point and no stadium crowd.
venues.anecdote_story: Wembley Park frames the trail as part of the area's reinvention into a creative district; recent additions include high-visibility murals and installations.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Murals, tile works, digital/light installations, photography and public artworks by local and international artists.
venues.safety_notes: Managed public realm; avoid major stadium ingress/egress and late-night quiet pockets.
venues.backup_venue: Second Floor Studios & Arts Wembley Park
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5-8 min walk, but only during open-studio/exhibition dates.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and Wembley crowd surges; check event calendar.

venues.venue_name: Second Floor Studios & Arts Wembley Park
venues.opening_hours: Open-studio/exhibition access is event-led; Brent Creates listed Summer Open Studios on the first Saturday of June 11am-5pm. Regular public opening UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-studio events appear open-access; non-event group visits UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good only on public open-studio dates.
venues.capacity_notes: Working studio community; not a drop-in gallery every day.
venues.why_fits_paints: The artist-studio setting is exactly the kind of making-in-public texture PAINTS likes. The problem is operational: public access is seasonal/event-based. Tier B because it is excellent on the right day and unusable on many others.
venues.anecdote_story: The studios opened at Wembley Park in 2018 as affordable, purpose-built space in a major regeneration district.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ceramics, costume design, drawing, fashion, animation, fine art, illustration, moving image, painting, photography and makers' studios during open events.
venues.safety_notes: Managed Wembley Park setting; avoid stadium crowd peaks.
venues.risks_watchouts: Must not be used without a confirmed public open-studio/exhibition date.

venues.opening_hours: Kiln food/drink page lists bar hours Monday, Tuesday, Thursday and Friday 5:30pm-11pm; Wednesday and Saturday 1pm-11pm, varying with events. Exhibition access UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bar/foyer likely walk-in around performances, but exhibition/group access UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible as exhibition-plus-bar if there is a live exhibition; needs host call.
venues.capacity_notes: Performance venue; crowd and access depend on show schedule.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday early afternoon before evening performance crowds.
venues.why_fits_paints: Kiln has deep local story and sometimes hosts visual exhibitions tied to Kilburn's communities. It does not consistently behave like a museum/gallery, so it is a Tier B conditional pick. Use only when a relevant exhibition is confirmed.
venues.anecdote_story: The theatre describes itself as world-class theatre from the heart of Kilburn; recent exhibition listings include Irish-Kilburn heritage work by Electronic Sheep and Harlesden Gallery network artists.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Event-led exhibitions, theatre foyer/bar, pizza and drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Kilburn High Road transit corridor; night departure should be direct to tube/overground/bus.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest Brent alternatives are Willesden Gallery/Brent Museum, about 20-25 min by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Conditional on exhibition schedule; no tight same-category backup.

venues.opening_hours: Official page gives address/contact and shop identity but live opening hours not found in official snippet; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in likely; 8-12 seated/two-hour coffee format not supported on-site, so Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Browse-and-discuss format possible; coffee must be paired nearby and seating is not guaranteed.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop with no verified in-house cafe; use as a browse anchor, then move to Salusbury Winestore/The Salusbury or another nearby independent cafe if confirmed.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday late morning before lunch crowds.
venues.why_fits_paints: The bookshop is culturally strong: independent, family-linked, 10,000+ curated titles and a Book Festival connection. It fails the strictest Books & Coffee requirement because coffee is not verified on-site or literally next door as a formal partner. Kept only as a Tier B Brent exception because otherwise the borough has almost no true category fit.
venues.anecdote_story: Queen's Park Books is the second Fergiani Books store; the first, West End Lane Books, dates to 1994, and the shop partners with Queen's Park Book Festival.
venues.serve_or_on_view: More than 10,000 hand-curated titles, classics to contemporary, events/book launches.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Queen's Park high street near tube/rail.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying bookshop-plus-coffee backup found within 10 min; closest cultural fallback is The Salusbury Winestore & Bar at about 1 min walk but it is not same category.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Does not fully meet Books & Coffee coffee/seating rule; host would need to relax the category or arrange nearby coffee.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Queen's Park is not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk parks/routes list, and the cafe was subject to 2026 operator-change controversy; do not source as Creative Walk or meeting point without team review.
Attractive Brent park but absent from the PAINTS v6 safety list; Creative Walks rule says do not source parks outside the approved list.
Not on the approved PAINTS safety list; also less suitable for strangers arriving/leaving individually than managed high-footfall routes.
Explicitly removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be used as primary route or backup.

Previously strong natural-wine/coffee candidate, but official social/web results indicate Queen's Park location's last trading day as Milk Beach was 22 March 2026 before renovation/new concept; operational identity not current enough.
Historically PAINTS-coded cocktail bar, but current operational status could not be verified and listings conflict, including a 'venue closed' signal; fails live reliability gate.
Excellent social-enterprise cinema and bar, but the category requires paintings, sculpture, textiles, ceramics or craft on view; cinema programming does not meet Museum & Gallery Days criteria.
High-footfall food hall/multi-operator box venue, but PAINTS brief rejects malls/templated venues; not a curated producer/maker market.
Shopping-centre/outlet environment; explicitly outside PAINTS aesthetic and market rules.
Beautiful community-library story and Mark Twain hook, but not a bookshop with on-site coffee or a confirmed literal coffee partner; fails Books & Coffee category.
The library/museum/gallery complex is valuable, but a current independent bookshop-plus-cafe offer could not be verified; historic Willesden Bookshop references are not enough for live sourcing.
